| Market Strategy Management | 1.1.1 | 2 | Market Strategy & Policy processes enable the development of a strategic view of an enterprise’s existing and desired market-place, activities and aims. | Leave Loyalty Program | 1.3.4.5.2.6 | 5 | Leave Loyalty Program begins and completes the process of leaving an enterprise's loyalty program. | Customer Problem Management | 1.3.7 | 2 | Customer problem management involves managing the capture and analysis of customer problems, identifying the root cause of the problem, correcting or providing solutions or compensation to customers, reporting and tracking customer problems, and ensuring that problems are resolved to customer satisfaction. | Isolate Customer Problem | 1.3.7.1 | 3 | Isolate Customer Problem business activity will verify the use of the product, perform diagnostics while liaising with the customer, and identify the root cause of the problem. | Assign Privileges to Parties | 1.6.3.1.7.2 | 5 | Assign Privileges to Parties is responsible to set the party's level of privileges. Note:  A parties' ability to interact with an organization is bounded by the privileges the organization assigns to the party.  While privileges are defined by strategy, policy and intent, they have a profound impact on the parties' experience of the organization, and thus need to be assigned with care.  Privileges are typically assigned to a parties identity, and are accessed from the context of that identity. |  | Business Partner Domain | Assurance |  | 3048 |  |
